message: Node started: Let D be a closed disk in the Euclidean plane R^2. Let the boundary of the disk, S^1, be partitioned into four disjoint open arcs Q_1, Q_2, Q_3, and Q_4, representing the four quadrants, such that the union of these arcs and their boundary points is S^1. Let X_1, X_2, ..., X_25 be a sequence of 25 random chords in D. Each chord X_i is defined by its two endpoints {P_{i,1}, P_{i,2}} on S^1. The chords are subject to the following constraints: 1) For each i in {1, ..., 25}, the endpoints P_{i,1} and P_{i,2} must belong to different quadrants (i.e., if P_{i,1} is in Q_j, then P_{i,2} is in Q_k where j is not equal to k). 2) The endpoints are chosen independently and uniformly at random from the union of the arcs, subject to the quadrant constraint. Let N be the random variable representing the number of regions into which the disk D is divided by the 25 chords. Compute the expected value E[N].
